thesis_bull: CSCO confirms rank 3/21 by 126-day total return (+25.61%) — comfortably inside the top-5 cohort with the SPY trend filter engaged. CSCO printed Q3 FY26 earnings AMC 2026-05-13 (we exited at $101.17 pre-print, capturing +$618.90 realized PnL). Today's signal layer treats CSCO as a re-entry candidate because there is no open position to suppress the ENTRY action — the rank-based momentum criterion is unchanged.
thesis_bear: Three stacking constraints make this an asymmetric NO_TRADE: (a) circuit-breaker state remains OUT (exposure_fraction=0.0) — new opens are mechanically blocked by Path Z policy with drawdown 15.37% vs persistent peak $119,140.25 (acknowledged-inflated artifact, seventh consecutive routine); (b) daily-bar data is stale to 2026-05-08 (6 calendar days) and therefore does NOT yet reflect post-earnings price action from 2026-05-13/05-14 — re-entering on a stale-bar rank is signal-staleness risk in its most direct form; (c) re-entering the same name within 24h of an earnings event would invert yesterday's overnight-risk reduction logic without new evidence about the post-print fundamentals. Any one constraint alone would prevent the open; together they reinforce the NO_TRADE.
invalidation_condition: CB returns to FULL or HALF state (exposure_fraction > 0.0) AND daily-bar feed catches up to within `data.max_data_staleness_seconds` (must include the post-earnings 2026-05-14 bar) AND post-earnings price action either confirms or repudiates the +$618.90 exit thesis. Until all three clear, this candidate stays in the watch column.

position_size.sizing_basis: Strategy B target 6% of $100k account ≈ $6,000 at IEX last $121.25 → 49 shares; throttled to 0 by CB OUT. Note: IEX post-close last_price ($121.25) carries a wide bid/ask ($109.35/$121.25) — re-entry must verify quote tightness during next-session market hours.

confidence_notes: Confidence in the NO_TRADE decision itself is high (≈0.95) — all three gates are mechanical. The 0.45 reflects below-neutral confidence in re-entering CSCO specifically because we just exited it ahead of earnings for a reason; without seeing post-earnings price action on a fresh bar, the rank signal alone does not warrant re-entry even if CB and staleness gates were clear.
risk_manager_notes: NO_TRADE reduces no risk and adds no exposure; the decision is APPROVED on the conservative side. If this had been a PAPER_BUY, risk-manager would have REJECTED on grounds: (a) circuit_breaker state=OUT throttles new opens to qty=0; (b) daily-bar staleness exceeds max_data_staleness_seconds=60 by orders of magnitude; (c) earnings-window proximity (post-AMC <24h) plus the explicit pre-print exit on 2026-05-13 creates a reversal-without-evidence pattern flagged by CLAUDE.md “any missing input is a reason to be more conservative”. Mechanical gates fail before the strategic gates matter.
compliance_notes: CSCO is on watchlist.yaml with approved_for_paper_trading=true; strategy large_cap_momentum_top5 is ACTIVE_PAPER_TEST; mode PAPER_TRADING permits all decision classes. NO_TRADE is recorded for prediction-review traceability and is the correct routing given the three mechanical/policy gates. Schema-compliant. No live execution.
